about.title: From idea to working product
about.description: I focus on building modern web applications using React, TypeScript, and Next.js, with strong attention to code quality, performance, and usability. I specialize in simplifying complex problems and turning them into intuitive user interfaces. I work closely with designers and stakeholders to ensure that delivered features are consistent, thoughtful, and truly useful.
highlights: In team environments, I focus on clear communication, solid organization, and continuous improvements that make collaboration smoother and more predictable.

items.description: Improved predictability and flow through process and impediment optimization.

items.label: client retention
items.description: Built long-term relationships and reliable value delivery as founder.

projects.title: Selected Work
projects.intro: Selected projects that reflect my approach to product development — from idea and design to delivery and real outcomes.
projects.planetHint: Each planet drifts slightly in place. Click one to open a large project preview without leaving this section.
projects.easterHint: Look at this section long enough and you might find an easter egg.
projects.easterEggModalBody: It is, of course, a UFO abducting a cow. Not just any UFO either, but one that clearly decided this portfolio needed one more dramatic subplot. The cow did not approve this roadmap item, but the cosmos rarely waits for stakeholder sign-off.

items.title: FUNKOLOGI
items.description: A full redesign and implementation for a psychologist's website, focused on trust, clearer positioning, accessibility, SEO, and a setup that supports easier self-managed content updates.
paragraphs: The starting point was straightforward: the client had previously built her own WordPress site, but the result did not communicate professionalism or trust. On top of that, she needed a setup that would let her add materials herself instead of relying on a developer for every update.

I approached the work as a mix of redesign, information architecture cleanup, and technical groundwork for future content growth. Beyond the visual layer, a key part of the delivery was building a concrete content flow: a public materials library with search, an admin area, authentication, and a form for uploading PDFs with metadata.

The final result was a modern Next.js website with stronger branding, clearer messaging, a structured materials library, and a working Firebase-backed admin flow. It was also built with accessibility, SEO, and long-term maintainability in mind. The key business outcome was simple: the client now has a site that looks credible and supports self-managed publishing and organizing of new resources.
bulletPoints: Moved the brand away from a cluttered self-built WordPress site into a polished custom frontend experience.
Designed a calm and trustworthy visual identity for families seeking specialist psychological support.
Simplified the content structure so visitors can quickly understand the offer, specialization, and next steps.
Designed a materials library with search, popular queries, and clear category cards.
Built an admin area with login, a published resources list, and edit/delete actions.
Added a PDF publishing flow with metadata, keywords, and file upload handling.
Connected Firebase Authentication, Storage, and Firestore as the content management backbone.
Built the project with accessibility, SEO, future growth, and scalable content operations in mind.

slides.title: Landing page / first impression
slides.caption: This screen shows the calm hero area with a strong headline, clear CTAs, and topic tags that immediately set the tone of the brand.

slides.title: Materials library and search
slides.caption: Here you can see the public materials section with search, popular query shortcuts, and category cards that help users reach the right content faster.

slides.title: Admin login
slides.caption: This view shows the lightweight login screen that gives the client access to the admin area for managing materials.

slides.title: Published materials dashboard
slides.caption: This screen highlights the actual content operations flow: a list of published PDFs, search, and edit/delete actions for each resource.
slides.imageAlt: Funcology article or content detail view

slides.title: Upload PDF modal
slides.caption: The final screenshot shows the publishing form with title, description, category, keywords, and file upload gathered into one clear workflow.
slides.imageAlt: Funcology detailed section in a narrow layout
